nodes.label: M001: Ownership, safety, and review baseline
S01: Pinemeter identity migration
T01: Renamed the build-critical Xcode project, scheme, app/test modules, source roots, and app entry point from ClaudeMeter to Pinemeter.
T02: Updated active product UI copy and metadata from ClaudeMeter to Pinemeter while preserving provider-specific Claude terminology.
T03: Created the Pinemeter identity exception map for persistent runtime identifiers and compatibility-sensitive names.
T04: Updated docs, site, workflows, demo script, and primary visual assets to Pinemeter identity.
T05: Ran final rename coverage scans and Xcode verification for the renamed Pinemeter project.
S02: Credential surface inventory
T01: Inventoried Keychain storage and settings persistence for Claude and ChatGPT credentials.
T02: Inventoried Claude session acquisition, validation, reuse, display, clearing, and recovery paths.
T03: Inventoried ChatGPT cookie acquisition, validation, token derivation, reuse, display, clearing, and recovery paths.
T04: Inventoried display, logging, error, test, and export exposure risks for credential material.
T05: Wrote the final credential/session surface inventory artifact for downstream security and auth planning.
S03: Security review baseline
T01: Added a security invariant test proving AppSettings UserDefaults persistence remains credential-free.
T02: Ranked Claude Keychain, SwiftUI credential-state, WebView session-retention, and logging redaction risks in the S03 assessment.
T03: Ranked ChatGPT cookie, Bearer-token, and generic error redaction risks and pinned user-facing disclosure invariants with synthetic sentinel tests.
T04: Finalized the S03 ranked security assessment with lifecycle cross-checks, downstream handoffs, gate sections, and focused XCTest evidence.
S04: Architecture review baseline
T01: Mapped current Pinemeter architecture boundaries for AppModel, provider services, settings persistence, credentials, and setup/settings views.
T02: Compared three provider-boundary alternatives and recommended avoiding a universal provider protocol for now.
T03: Ranked architecture findings and created downstream handoffs for S05, S06, M002, and later provider milestones.
S05: Provider and error workflow audit
T01: Added a fixed-allowlist provider workflow copy audit harness for source and public-doc drift checks.
T02: Qualified Claude credential errors, setup/settings recovery copy, and popover recovery-button detection with focused regression tests.
T03: Redacted NetworkService HTTP and decode failure diagnostics while adding a source-level security invariant against response-body and credential-fragment logging.
T04: Updated public provider positioning and wrote the S05 provider/error workflow assessment with passing audit and focused test verification.
S06: Safe cleanup and ownership refactor
T01: CacheRepository now writes Pinemeter-owned cache/export paths while preserving ClaudeMeter legacy compatibility.
T02: Guarded legacy ClaudeMeter credential identifiers with source comments and executable source-level invariants.
T03: AppSettings refresh interval clamping now uses shared Constants.Refresh bounds with focused boundary tests.
T04: Aligned the tracked working-status document with current Pinemeter ownership while preserving historical and operational ClaudeMeter exceptions.
S07: Verification and open source history plan
T01: Captured fresh passing renamed Pinemeter Xcode test and clean build evidence for final milestone verification.
T02: Created the final S07 identity and public hygiene audit classifying remaining legacy references, missing public-readiness files, and redacted secret-shaped scan findings.
T03: Wrote the non-destructive Pinemeter open-source history and public hygiene plan with explicit human confirmation gates.
T04: Assembled the final S07 assessment tying Pinemeter build/test evidence, final audit classifications, and the non-destructive open-source history plan to M001 requirements.
M002: Durable credential acquisition
S01: Credential state contract
T01: Added a provider credential state domain model with sanitized health and failure descriptions.
T02: Added a non-secret credential status service boundary for Claude and ChatGPT credentials.
T03: Added regression tests that keep AppSettings and SettingsRepository free of credential state and credential material persistence.
S02: Claude Keychain repair flow
T01: Added an explicit Claude session key repair API to the Keychain repository with typed create/update outcomes.
T02: Wired Claude credential repair through the session import service and AppModel state.
T03: Added SecurityInvariantTests coverage and durable knowledge for the Claude Keychain prompt repair path under the official Autimo signed app identity.
S03: ChatGPT session acquisition boundary
T01: Added a Keychain-backed ChatGPT session repository boundary with transient access-token handling and sanitized acquisition diagnostics.
T02: Connected ChatGPT WebView/session acquisition and usage refresh flows to the secure ChatGPT session repository boundary.
T03: Added ChatGPT credential redaction invariant tests for persisted acquisition diagnostics, AppSettings separation, and user-facing error copy.
S04: Credential setup and recovery UX
T01: Added sanitized provider credential status view models to AppModel for Claude and ChatGPT credential recovery UI.
T02: Added Settings credential recovery rows for Claude and optional ChatGPT using sanitized provider status view models.
T03: Updated the setup wizard to use sanitized durable credential status for ready, missing, and repairable Claude session states.
S05: Credential lifecycle verification
T01: Added credential lifecycle regression tests across Claude and ChatGPT paths.
T02: Ran the full Debug credential verification suite and confirmed official Autimo signing settings remain intact.
T03: Validated R010 with M002/S05 lifecycle evidence and recorded M003 handoff scope for provider workflow polish.
M003: Multi-provider workflow polish
S01: Provider status surfaces
T01: Repaired T01's verification contract so auto-mode can discover and run host-owned checks.
T02: Unified provider credential status presentation text for setup and settings without exposing credential material.
T03: Rendered shared provider credential status actions in setup and pinned setup/settings status surfaces to the sanitized shared model.
T04: Ran full provider status verification and confirmed setup/settings credential status surfaces remain sanitized.
T05: Reconciled the provider status surface audit with the final shared sanitized setup/settings presentation contract.
S02: Provider recovery actions
T01: Mapped current Claude and ChatGPT recovery action entry points, target provider action API gaps, and safety risks for S02 follow-up implementation.
T02: Centralized provider credential recovery actions in AppModel and routed setup repair/clear flows through that sanitized orchestration boundary.
T03: Wired provider recovery buttons in Settings and Setup through the shared AppModel provider credential action boundary with provider-scoped sanitized feedback.
T04: Verified provider recovery workflows, provider-specific copy, and credential redaction invariants without requiring source changes.
S03: Menu bar multi-provider usage
T01: Audited menu bar provider assumptions and documented the current display-state matrix for no-provider, Claude-only, ChatGPT-only, both-provider, loading, and error states.
T02: Added provider-aware menu usage state so ChatGPT-only and mixed-provider setups route to the usage popover with provider-specific titles, loading text, refresh behavior, and tests.
T03: Added regression coverage for provider-aware menu state, hidden ChatGPT usage, unavailable ChatGPT storage, and ChatGPT refresh demotion when credentials disappear.
T04: Verified the menu bar multi-provider behavior with full-suite tests and deterministic copy scans, with no scope-relevant code changes required.
S04: Workflow UAT and diagnostics
T01: Wrote the provider workflow UAT checklist for reset, partial-provider, full-provider, expired-session, and clear/reconnect states.
T02: Added safe automated reset and redaction checks for provider credential workflows.
T03: Ran the final M003 verification suite and fixed the stale provider status audit test-name guard.
T04: Recorded non-destructive M003 UAT evidence and milestone readiness notes.
M004: Gemini monitoring extension
S01: Gemini provider contract
T01: Mapped the current Claude and ChatGPT provider seams that Gemini must neutralize before implementation.
T02: Added Gemini provider credential contract coverage and minimal provider status support.
T03: Verified provider contract compatibility after Gemini contract-state additions.
S02: Gemini credential and usage service
T01: Defined the Gemini credential boundary as an API-key Keychain repository/service seam with sanitized diagnostics and no AppSettings persistence.
T02: Implemented Gemini API key repository and usage service with sanitized diagnostics and tests.
T03: Integrated Gemini API-key backed usage state into AppModel refresh, credential status, and clear/reconnect surfaces with focused AppModel coverage.
S03: Gemini setup and settings UI
T01: Extended shared provider credential status behavior and setup/settings copy so Gemini API-key status appears beside Claude and ChatGPT without exposing credential material.
T02: Added Gemini recovery UI and model tests for missing, configured, invalid, retry, reconnect, clear, and mixed-provider behavior.
T03: Verified setup/settings provider copy and aligned ChatGPT bootstrap tests with the Gemini-aware provider matrix.
S04: Gemini menu usage integration
T01: Added AppModel popover-content state that accounts for Gemini usage and Gemini errors.
T02: Gemini usage and error states now render in the menu popover alongside configured providers.
T03: Added AppModel regression tests for Gemini-only, mixed Gemini provider combinations, all-provider display state, and Gemini refresh error display state.
S05: Gemini workflow UAT
T01: Created a repeatable Gemini workflow UAT checklist for setup, refresh, failure recovery, Gemini-only, and all-provider states.
T02: Ran final Gemini verification and resolved enforced provider copy findings.
T03: Recorded Gemini UAT evidence with automated artifact checks, targeted runtime regression proof, and explicit human-only boundaries.
T04: Fixed the copyable provider-error regression by making setup credential-card failure titles copyable and updating the regression test for the shared ChatGPT/Gemini provider error row.
M005: Public open-source polish
S01: Public docs accuracy pass
S02: Contributor templates and support paths
S03: Release and signing documentation
S04: Fresh-reader public UAT
S01: VM automation access harness
S02: Clean install and first run reset
S03: Computer use onboarding import
S04: Runtime state and diagnostic verification
S06: Reusable validation command and handoff

Comprehensive identity ownership was the right M001 boundary: Pinemeter now owns the app/project/scheme/module/source/test/docs/site identity wherever feasible, while compatibility-sensitive identifiers were classified instead of blindly renamed.

Durable credential acquisition was intentionally deferred: M001 produced inventory, security review, and invariants, while M002 remains responsible for safer app-owned acquisition, fallback migration, and replace-not-display flows.
Architecture review could proceed as an artifact-backed local baseline when external advisor capacity was unavailable, as long as findings were ranked and handed to cleanup/provider-boundary work.

Destructive git history and publication actions require fresh human approval: M001 produced a plan and explicitly avoided filter-repo, reset, remote pushes, releases, site deployment, repository creation, and secret-store mutation.

Public ownership readiness should be sequenced after safety and verification: M001 validated ownership/review baseline, leaving durable credentials, provider-aware workflows, Gemini monitoring, and launch-grade public polish for later milestones.

Retained `claudemeter` Keychain/cache/export/access-group identifiers are compatibility surfaces, not failed rename work; changing them without migration could orphan existing user credentials or usage state.

Credential risk is not limited to durable settings persistence: M001 established that SettingsRepository remains preference-only, while risk remains in transient UI/app state, Keychain, WebView cookies, ChatGPT session material, and diagnostics.
Provider/error copy can safely improve before full provider redesign when changes are narrow, source-reviewed, and covered by focused XCTest evidence.

Cleanup work must preserve compatibility and security invariants; S06 proved stale ownership cleanup with cache/export compatibility, credential invariants, settings clamp behavior, provider copy, session keys, and redacted diagnostics.

A passing milestone validation still needs current closeout freshness checks: code-change diff evidence, slice completion status, summaries, and requirement transitions were rechecked before marking M001 complete.
Use build-critical rename first, then classify residual old-identity references as compatibility, history, or release-planning exceptions before downstream inventory and review.
Credential inventory tables should map account/material/writer/readers/clearer/service attributes so security review and migration planning can proceed without rediscovery.
Cross-slice review baselines should produce ranked findings plus executable invariants; later cleanup slices can then make safe fixes without widening scope.
Non-destructive public-readiness planning should explicitly list forbidden actions alongside the plan so automatic agents do not rewrite history, push remotes, publish releases, or mutate secrets.
Some of the most important remaining old names are deliberately correct because they preserve existing user data and credentials rather than indicating missed search-and-replace work.
The initial app ownership milestone surfaced enough credential and provider workflow risk to split durable credentials, provider-aware UX, and Gemini support into separate later milestones.
A mostly artifact-based milestone still required executable proof for safety-sensitive cleanup and provider/error changes, not just narrative review reports.
